methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $29.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Stark Defence
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Reveal Technology is a Series A–stage defense and security company founded by Garrett Smith that has raised $30M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 10 comparable companies (2 public, 8 private) operating in the same sector and stage, including Scale AI, Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $30M raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, implies a $60M valuation. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $400M, though the peer set exhibits wide dispersion—ranging from $150M (Ares Industries) to $29.0B (Scale AI)—reflecting meaningful heterogeneity within the frontier-defense category.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $51M (low) to $460M (high), with a median of $230M. The wide band reflects the material uncertainty inherent in the peer set: Scale AI's substantial outlier valuation elevates the peer median considerably, while stage-progression methodology anchors to the company's actual capital raised. Key uncertainties include whether Reveal's technical differentiation and market position track toward the upper tier of the peer cohort (Stark Defence at $500M) or remain closer to the lower quartile. Revenue traction, customer concentration, and intellectual property scope warrant detailed diligence to narrow this range.
